title: Phenol Chloroform DNA Extraction from Polycarbonate Filters
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ibbcain

steps.instruction: If not using a whole filter, aseptically cut out a piece (e.g. a 1/2) of a filter. For aseptic technique, dip steel forceps and scissor into 95% ethanol, then flame forceps. Repeat twice.
Put filter into sterile polypropylene tube.
Place tube on ice.
Add lysis buffer into tubes. See "Before you start" on options for a lysis buffer.
Massage the filter with a flame-sterilized glass rod to suspend all the cells from the filter.
Add lysozyme from a 100 mg/mL stock to a final concentration of 1 mg/mL.
Incubate 20 minutes at 37ºC Ideally on a shaker, but a water bath is ok.
Add proteinase K (20 mg/ml stock solution). Final concentration of 50 μg/ml.
Add 10% SDS, final concentration 0.5%.
Incubate 2 hours at 50ºC. Ideally on a shaker, but a water bath is ok.
Do all of the following in the fume hood.
Add saturated phenol into tubes, 1/2 of the total aqueous volume. Saturated phenol can be purchased, rather than made, if preferred.
Cap the tubes tightly, shake the contents well. Allow it to sit for 5 minutes.
Same as phenol, add equal volume of chloroform isoamyl alcohol (2.5 mL) and repeat step 2. On a tabletop centrifuge, run for 1 minute on maximum speed.
Transfer the aqueous (top) layter into sterile Oak Ridge-tubes. Oak Ridge tubes.
Add 2x the aqueous volume of ice cold absolute ethanol into tubes.
Add 0.1x the total volume of 10 M ammonium acetate.
Mix the contents and place the tubes in -20ºC freezer for at least 1 hour, or overnight.
Spin the tubes at 10,000 rpm for 35 minutes.
Carefully remove the supernatant.
Carefully wash the DNA pellet with 1 mL of ice-cold 70% ethanol.
Remove the 70% ethanol and place the tubes in sterile hood and let the DNA pellets dry for 2-3 hours, until completely dry.
Resuspend the DNA into 200 μL of sterile TE buffer. (pH 8)
Measure the DNA concentration using the spectrophotometer and/or fluorometer. https://www.protocols.io/view/nanodrop-spectrophotometer-nd-1000-for-nucleic-aci-id2ca8e
Store DNA in a -20ºC freezer.
